# Table

Presents related records in responsive, semantic rows and columns.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Semantic structure](#semantic-structure)
- [Empty state](#empty-state)
- [Responsive layout](#responsive-layout)
- [Data and presentation boundary](#data-and-presentation-boundary)
- [When to use](#when-to-use)

## Installation

Add Table from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/table
```

## Usage

Compose the same structure as native table markup: `TableHeader` and `TableBody` contain `TableRow` elements, while `TableHead` and `TableCell` represent column headings and data cells.

```vue
<template>
  <Table>
    <TableCaption>A list of recent invoices.</TableCaption>
    <TableHeader>
      <TableRow>
        <TableHead>Invoice</TableHead>
        <TableHead>Status</TableHead>
        <TableHead class="text-right">Amount</TableHead>
      </TableRow>
    </TableHeader>
    <TableBody>
      <TableRow v-for="invoice in invoices" :key="invoice.id">
        <TableCell class="font-medium">{{ invoice.id }}</TableCell>
        <TableCell><Badge variant="secondary">{{ invoice.status }}</Badge></TableCell>
        <TableCell class="text-right">{{ invoice.amount }}</TableCell>
      </TableRow>
    </TableBody>
  </Table>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Badge } from '@/Components/Badge'
import {
  Table,
  TableBody,
  TableCaption,
  TableCell,
  TableHead,
  TableHeader,
  TableRow,
} from '@/Components/Table'

const invoices = [
  { id: 'INV001', status: 'Paid', amount: '€250.00' },
  { id: 'INV002', status: 'Pending', amount: '€150.00' },
  { id: 'INV003', status: 'Paid', amount: '€350.00' },
]
</script>
```

## Semantic structure

`Table` renders a native `table` inside a responsive overflow container. Use `TableCaption` to identify the dataset, keep header labels concise, and put totals or summaries in `TableFooter`. Do not reproduce a table with generic grid or flex elements when column relationships matter.

The local head and cell primitives forward classes but do not add custom scope or header associations. For complex multi-level headers, add the required native attributes or use purpose-built markup that preserves correct `th`/`td` relationships.

## Empty state

Render `TableEmpty` inside `TableBody` and set `colspan` to the number of visible columns. Name the reason for absence—such as active filters—without replacing the table's caption or headers.

**Empty example**

```vue
<template>
  <Table>
    <TableCaption>Projects visible to your team.</TableCaption>
    <TableHeader>
      <TableRow>
        <TableHead>Name</TableHead>
        <TableHead>Owner</TableHead>
        <TableHead>Status</TableHead>
      </TableRow>
    </TableHeader>
    <TableBody>
      <TableEmpty :colspan="3">
        No projects match the current filters.
      </TableEmpty>
    </TableBody>
  </Table>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Table,
  TableBody,
  TableCaption,
  TableEmpty,
  TableHead,
  TableHeader,
  TableRow,
} from '@/Components/Table'
</script>
```

## Responsive layout

Wide tables scroll horizontally inside the Table container. Preserve the most important columns first, keep numeric columns aligned, and avoid hiding essential context solely to prevent scrolling. On very narrow screens, an Item list may communicate a small record set better than a table with many truncated columns.

## Data and presentation boundary

These components present already prepared rows. Fetching, filtering, sorting, pagination, selection, permissions, formatting, and mutations belong to the parent feature. Keep stable row keys on repeated `TableRow` elements and format dates, numbers, currency, and statuses before or while rendering cells.

## When to use

- Use Table when readers compare values across consistent columns or need exact data.
- Use Chart when trend, distribution, or relative magnitude is the primary question.
- Use Item for responsive records that do not need aligned columns.

Hosted documentation: [Table](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/table)
